ddls:
CREATE TABLE "individuals" (
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each individual */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the individual */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the individual */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the individual */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the individual */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the individual */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the individual */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual's occupation */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual's education level */,
  "income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the individual */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the individual was registered in the system */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the individual's record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_income FOREIGN KEY ("income") REFERENCES income_levels ("income_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_individuals_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the occupation */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the occupation */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* Type of employment (e.g., full-time, part-time) */,
  "job_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the occupation */,
  "occupation_score" INTEGER /* Score assigned to the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_name" TEXT /* Name of the education level */,
  "degree" TEXT /* Degree or certification */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Educational institution */,
  "graduation_year" INTEGER /* Year of graduation */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study */,
  "education_score" INTEGER /* Score assigned to the education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "income_levels" (
  "income_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income level */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Range of the income level */,
  "tax_bracket" TEXT /* Tax bracket for the income level */,
  "net_income" INTEGER /* Net income after taxes */,
  "source_of_income" TEXT /* Source of income (e.g., salary, investments) */,
  "income_score" INTEGER /* Score assigned to the income level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "socioeconomic_classes" (
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each socioeconomic class */,
  "class_name" TEXT /* Name of the socioeconomic class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the socioeconomic class */,
  "average_income" INTEGER /* Average income of individuals in the class */,
  "average_education_score" INTEGER /* Average education score of individuals in the class */,
  "average_occupation_score" INTEGER /* Average occupation score of individuals in the class */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "kuppuswamy_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Kuppuswamy score */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual */,
  "kuppuswamy_score" INTEGER /* Kuppuswamy score of the individual */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual's socioeconomic class */,
  "score_date" TEXT /* Date when the score was calculated */,
  "score_reason" TEXT /* Reason or notes for the score calculation */,
  "previous_score" INTEGER /* Previous Kuppuswamy score for comparison */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kuppuswamy_scores_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_kuppuswamy_scores_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES socioeconomic_classes ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the policy */,
  "class_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the targeted socioeconomic class */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the policy */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy becomes effective */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy ends */,
  "budget" INTEGER /* Budget allocated for the policy */,
  "target_population" TEXT /* Target population (e.g., age group, gender) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policies_class_id FOREIGN KEY ("class_id") REFERENCES socioeconomic_classes ("class_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_impact" (
  "impact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy impact */,
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the policy */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the individual affected by the policy */,
  "impact_date" TEXT /* Date when the policy impact was recorded */,
  "impact_type" TEXT /* Type of impact (e.g., financial support, educational assistance) */,
  "amount" INTEGER /* Amount of financial support or other resources provided */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the impact (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "follow_up_needed" INTEGER /* Indicates if follow-up is required */,
  "follow_up_date" TEXT /* Date for follow-up */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("impact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_impact_individual_id FOREIGN KEY ("individual_id") REFERENCES individuals ("individual_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_impact_policy_id FOREIGN KEY ("policy_id") REFERENCES policies ("policy_id")
)
